arxiv_requirements_snapshot.submission_root_rule: arXiv compiles from the root of the submitted directory even when the top-level TeX file is in a subdirectory.

recommended_pipeline.purpose: Build the generated LaTeX source under the arXiv default TeX Live generation before packaging.
recommended_pipeline.notes: If TeX Live 2025 is unavailable locally, record the missing toolchain as an external gate.

recommended_pipeline.purpose: Collect or flatten LaTeX dependencies when the generated source uses includes, subfiles, or bibliography workflows that need explicit packaging.
recommended_pipeline.notes: Use arxiv-collector when dependency discovery is more important; use latexpand when a single expanded TeX file is required.

recommended_pipeline.purpose: Create the default cleaned arXiv upload directory with comments, unused files, and avoidable oversized assets removed.
recommended_pipeline.notes: This is the default sanitizer for the FOI-O arXiv package.

recommended_pipeline.purpose: Optionally compare a stricter sanitization pass when it preserves the compiled PDF and does not remove required assets.
recommended_pipeline.notes: Use only as an additional check because the upstream tool explicitly warns that users assume responsibility for safety and stability.

recommended_pipeline.purpose: Scan the cleaned source package for secrets, local paths, comments, stale generated files, PDF metadata, and unintended source disclosure.
recommended_pipeline.tool: gitleaks or trufflehog plus exiftool, qpdf, and pdfinfo where applicable
recommended_pipeline.notes: The package remains a human-submission artifact; passing scans does not imply upload approval.

tool_checks.expected_output: A clean PDF build log from the generated LaTeX source.
A collected source directory containing the top-level TeX file and required dependencies.
A flattened TeX source when the package target requires a single expanded file.
A cleaned arXiv upload directory suitable for zip packaging and rebuild checks.
A stricter cleaned source package that preserves the generated PDF when the tool is available and stable.
No secrets, credentials, or local-only sensitive paths in the cleaned upload package.
PDF metadata and font checks recorded when PDFs are included in the source or supplement package.

package_checks.reviewer_notes: The cleaned arXiv source must rebuild before it is marked ready.
All paths in the upload package must work from the submission root.
Cleaned source must not include comments, internal review notes, draft-only data, or hidden local paths.

human_gates: Confirm arXiv category and subject classification.
Confirm author list, affiliations, funding, conflicts, and submission authority.
Approve final arXiv upload after local package validation.
